title.rendered: Willie Nelson and Emmylou Harris: Dry Lightning

content.rendered:
Here’s another cover I like better than the original.

Willie Nelson and Emmylou Harris infuse “Dry Lightning’ with more authentic world-weariness than Bruce manages to convey on his own, and the mariachi melody that’s understated in the original is prominent in this cover–transporting us immediately to the border where this song is set.

© July 23, 2018

excerpt.rendered: Willie Nelson and Emmylou Harris hit a home run with their world-weary take on “Dry Lightning.”

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, Wainright Family, Emmylou Harris, Brandi Carlile, and more: Hungry Heart
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: In February 2017, the Cayamo festival-at-sea featured some terrific team-ups. Here's one of them: "Hungry Heart" covered by the Wainright family, Emmylou Harris, Brandi Carlile, and more.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Linda Ronstadt, Emmylou Harris, and Neil Young: Across the Border
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Three great voices blend lovingly in this gorgeous arrangement of "Across the Border."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Spanish Dancer
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: During a week of holiday shows in 2001, Patti Scialfa performed her beautiful 1993 ballad with her husband backing her on guitar.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: Satan’s Jeweled Crown
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: In the spring of 1993, Bruce leaned further into his new band's strengths with their gorgeous cover of a country-gospel classic.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Scott Jacobs: Dry Lightning
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Canadian folk singer Marc Jacobs finds the soul of Bruce's "Dry Lightning" and turns in a beautiful cover on a gray Vancouver day.

jetpack-related-posts.title: October 28
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce's Bridge School Benefit sequel, the E Street Band's first-ever show, the DVD release that never was, and more--all from this date.
